commands.review_summary:
The PR routes zsh option descriptions through a dedicated double-quoted-description escaper and adds regression assertions for `$OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR` and backtick command examples.

PR surface: Source +5, Tests +6. Total +11 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source-level. Current main still emits `$` and backticks from option descriptions into generated zsh completion specs, and the contributor's screenshots show the before/after `openclaw models <TAB>` behavior.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] No cleanup close applies; maintainer review should decide whether to land this canonical narrow fix as-is or fold in sibling subcommand-description escaping.

target.title: CLI: escape zsh completion descriptions
target.body: ## Summary ### Problem Zsh completion descriptions are emitted inside double-quoted `_arguments` specs, but `$` and backticks were not escaped. This means descriptions such as `$OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR/completions` or ``models status --json`` can be interpreted by zsh instead of being displayed literally. ### Why it matters This can break or corrupt CLI tab-completion output and degrades the developer experience. ### What changed - Escaped `$` in zsh completion option descriptions - Escaped backticks in zsh completion option descriptions - Added regression tests for both cases ### What did NOT change - No changes to CLI behavior - No changes to model functionality - No unrelated changes --- ## Change Type * [x] Bug fix * [x] Refactor required for the fix --- ## Scope * [x] UI / DX --- ## Root Cause * Invalid completion dispatch using `$line[1]` caused `models` to be executed as a command --- ## User-visible Changes * `openclaw models ` no longer shows errors * Subcommands are correctly suggested --- ## Repro ```bash openclaw models ``` Before: ``` command not found: models ``` After: ``` (subcommands listed) ``` --- ## Compatibility * Backward compatible: Yes
target.author: edenkangdw
